purpose: Encodes the Senior Data Analyst role profile, biasing a worker toward SaaS metrics rigor: precise metric definitions (formula/source/owner/cadence), leading-vs-lagging indicator framing, segmentation discipline, statistical honesty (sample size, significance), and actionability. A master assigns this profile when a task involves designing KPI frameworks, dashboard specs, cohort analyses, or any data-driven recommendation document. The profile creates strong constraints against vanity metrics, correlation-causation conflation, and dashboards with more than 5-7 KPIs, and prescribes a specific document skeleton (Business Goals → Metric Categories → Definitions table → Dashboard Layout → Alert Thresholds → Review Cadence).
gotchas: Worker will refuse to ship a metric without a precise calculation/formula table — masters must allow time for definition rigor.
Profile prohibits dashboards with >5-7 metrics per view; do not request 'comprehensive metric dashboards' without segmenting into multiple views.
Insists on actionability: every observation must be paired with a recommendation, which can balloon report length if scope is wide.
patterns: Five-section profile spine: Role → Priorities (Ranked) → Constraints (Do NOT list) → Output Style → Success Criteria → Context Gathering.
Output format prescribes definition tables (metric, formula, source, owner, cadence) for every KPI.
conventions: Filename matches role slug; H1 'Profile: <Role Name>'.
Numbered priorities with bold lead-phrase, numbered constraints with 'Do NOT' prefix.
Trailing 'Context Gathering' steps tell the worker to read existing analytics implementations and KPI docs first.
